description_long: This is a well-worn, vintage-style baseball glove made primarily of tan and brown leather, showing signs of extensive use with visible creases, scuffs, and darkened areas from handling. The glove features four distinct finger stalls and a thumb stall, each padded and separated, with reinforced stitching along the edges and seams. The webbing between the thumb and index finger is a closed basket style, tightly laced for durability and flexibility. Prominent lacing is visible along the perimeter, fingers, and webbing, with a cross-laced pattern securing the structure. The palm area displays the 'Spalding' brand logo and model information stamped in black ink, while the back of the glove shows additional stitching and a partially open wrist area. The overall shape is contoured to fit a hand, with a deep pocket for catching baseballs and a slightly flared opening at the wrist.
description: A vintage tan and brown leather baseball glove with visible wear, closed basket webbing, and prominent cross-lacing. The glove features four finger stalls, a thumb stall, and a stamped Spalding logo on the palm.
description_view_0: Front view showing the deep pocket, finger stalls, thumb stall, and the Spalding logo stamped on the palm.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the thickness of the glove, the lacing along the edge, and the curvature of the finger stalls.
description_view_2: Back view displaying the closed basket webbing, reinforced stitching, and the open wrist area.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile emphasizing the lacing along the thumb and the overall contour of the glove.
attribute_finger_stalls: Four finger stalls and one thumb stall, each padded and separated, with rounded tips and reinforced stitching.
attribute_lacing_style: Cross-laced pattern visible on the webbing, fingers, and perimeter, providing structural integrity and flexibility.
attribute_brand_logo: Spalding logo visible on the palm in black ink, with model information stamped nearby.
attribute_wrist_strap_design: Partially open wrist area with no visible closure mechanism; appears to rely on the glove's shape for fit.
